mainEntity.name: Do I need a managed switch for VLANs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. If you want to carry multiple VLANs over one cable and assign ports to different VLANs, you need a managed switch or smart switch with VLAN support.

mainEntity.name: Can an unmanaged switch pass VLAN tags?

acceptedAnswer.text: Some unmanaged switches may pass tagged frames, but they cannot properly assign access ports, manage PVIDs, or enforce VLAN membership. A managed switch is recommended.

mainEntity.name: Should my trunk port be tagged or untagged?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a clean router-to-switch trunk, tag every VLAN unless you specifically need a native VLAN.

mainEntity.name: What is the CPU or self port in LuCI?

acceptedAnswer.text: The CPU or self entry represents the router side of the bridge. It must be tagged for VLANs that OpenWrt needs to route, firewall, or provide DHCP for.

mainEntity.name: Can I use the same trunk for access points?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. If your access point supports VLAN-tagged SSIDs, connect it to a switch port configured as a trunk and tag the SSID VLANs on that port.
